page: Amazon Web Services r8g.medium is a memory-optimized instance powered by a custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor built on the arm64 architecture. This instance features 1 dedicated physical core and 1 vCPU running at 2.8 GHz, supported by 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. No GPU accelerators are included.

Benchmark results indicate a clear division between single-core capabilities and multi-core or high-throughput operations. The single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the average middle 50% category. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show strong performance in the top 25% tier. However, due to the single-core constraint, multi-core benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and Passmark CPU scores rank in the bottom 10% to 25% ranges.

The r8g.medium offers a cost-efficient entry point into the Graviton8 memory-optimized family, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without the overhead of larger multi-core instances. The primary tradeoff is the limited single-vCPU compute capacity, which restricts its suitability for parallel processing or high-throughput database operations. This instance is best suited for lightweight memory-bound workloads, small-scale caching layers, single-threaded data processing, and development or testing environments requiring arm64 compatibility.
description: Amazon Web Services r8g.medium is a memory-optimized instance powered by a single-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 1 dedicated vCPU, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Single-core benchmarks, such as Geekbench and stress-ng, place the instance in the average performance tier, while single-threaded compression tasks show strong results. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower percentiles due to the single-vCPU limitation. This configuration offers a cost-efficient entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel compute power. It is best suited for lightweight caching, single-threaded data processing, and arm64 development environments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.medium is a memory-optimized arm64 instance featuring 1 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 core, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, ideal for lightweight memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.medium is a memory-optimized arm64 instance with 1 vCPU, 8.0 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps network speed, running on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A single-core, memory-optimized arm64 instance designed for lightweight, memory-bound workloads and single-threaded data processing tasks.
bullet_points: Powered by a single-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 processor at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and 36 MB L3 cache
Delivers strong single-threaded compression and decompression performance
Features a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for lightweight caching, single-threaded processing, and arm64 testing
